source_jobs.title: Senior Project Engineer
source_jobs.description:
COMPANY OVERVIEW

TransGrid Energy is a renewable energy company focused on investing, developing, owning and operating utility-scale battery energy storage systems and solar projects. TransGrid has a robust pipeline of projects in the near term, and it will expand its energy asset portfolio across the United States.

As an affiliate company of Hanwha Group, a FORTUNE Global 500 company driven by a firm commitment to advancing the clean energy future, TransGrid Energy is strived to shape the renewable energy landscape by providing a sustainable and reliable power solutions tailored to meet the energy needs of today and tomorrow.

POSITION OVERVIEW

TransGrid Energy is seeking an ambitious and passionate Senior Project Engineer to join our Team and will be responsible for leading and working with EPC Partners, Transmission Providers, IOUs and TGE’s off-take customers to ensure successful construction, commissioning, and operating utility-scale battery energy storage systems and solar projects. TGE has partnered with engineering contractors and this position is responsible for engaging and coordinating on all stages of project design, installation, and commissioning.

RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Lead and review technical documentation such as site studies, reports, permits, etc. as related to design, cost or schedule impact.

 - Review technical components of project contracts (EPCA, Procurement Contracts, LTSA, PPA, IA, etc.).

 - Develop specifications, contracts, and a document control structure to support existing projects and the future pipeline.

 - Manage workflows of 3rd party Owner’s Engineers.

 - Create, review, and maintain drawing packages, energy production models, bills of materials, scopes of work and other technical project documentation.

 - Manage the technical needs in all stages of project development and design, including conceptual and advanced system design, observing regulatory codes as well as customer requirements, specify balance of systems, perform site visits, coordinate needs for the permitting and technical support during the construction phase.

 - Implement external resources such as structural, civil and electrical engineer consultants, geotechnical and topographic surveyors, other specialized professionals as needed and suppliers.

 - Review conceptual design for ESS and solar projects including selection of major components, equipment layouts, single-line diagrams, etc.

 - Prepare bid packages for contractors including scope of work, schedule, project description, and conceptual design.

 - Lead the technical tasks in preparation of EPC agreement, Exhibits, and the review of potential project acquisition opportunities.

 - Lead and supervise in the field with site visits as needed, testing, commissioning and start-up of constructed power plants.

 - In collaboration with other team members, contribute to the completion of proposals to design and deliver BESS and solar projects.

 - Work with O&M provider and be responsible for technical requirements of battery and solar performance.

 - Work with O&M and optimizer partners to track, monitor, and coordinate with Asset Management Team to enhance performance of battery and solar resources.

 - Act as the primary point of contact with equipment vendors.

 - Maintain continuous improvements in project designs, plans, processes and product development.

 - Lead and supervise as needed, testing, operating and maintenance for asset.

 - Manage the technical obligations according to PPA.

 - Coordinate internal and external resources for subject matter expertise and project risk reduction.

 - Perform other duties and special projects, as assigned.

REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - A degree in Electrical Engineering.

 - Minimum of 7+ years of professional engineering experience in the utility scale battery energy storage system and solar industry.

 - Relevant work experience in the design and development of utility scale solar and battery storage power plants.

source_jobs.title: Senior Accountant
source_jobs.description:
COMPANY OVERVIEW

TransGrid Energy is a newly established renewable energy company to invest, develop, own and operate utility-scale renewable energy projects. TransGrid has a robust pipeline of projects in the near term, and it will expand its energy asset portfolio across the United States.

As an affiliate company of Hanwha Group, a FORTUNE Global 500 company driven by a firm commitment to advancing the clean energy future, TransGrid Energy is strived to shape the renewable energy landscape by providing sustainable and reliable power solutions tailored to meet the energy needs of today and tomorrow.

POSITION OVERVIEW

The Accounting Senior role is a key position within the finance department, reporting to the Senior Accounting Manager. You'll be involved in a wide range of accounting activities, including month-end closing and financial reporting. A crucial part of this role is dealing with specific, complex accounting areas for the renewable energy industry, like tax equity HLBV accounting and lease accounting.

RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Assist and participate in the preparation of various financial statements and reports, ensuring the integrity and quality of financial reporting in accordance with GAAP.

 - Perform month-end close functions, including the maintenance of general ledgers, accounts receivable, revenue, cash, depreciation, cost, property, revenues, operating expenses, and borrowings.

 - Assist in the development and implementation of goals, policies, priorities, and procedures related to financial management, budgeting, and accounting. Ensure compliance with established policies and procedures.

 - Assist in ensuring compliance with Sarbanes-Oxley (SOX) requirements and maintain robust internal controls over financial reporting.

 - Contribute to the continuous improvement of accounting processes and compliance matters

 - Assist in establishing and resolving complex accounting procedures and processes specific to renewable energy.

 - Record and reconcile loan origination, funding, interest accruals, fees, and repayments for project financing transactions.

 - Perform other duties and special projects as assigned.

REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - Bachelor’s degree in accounting, finance, or a related field

 - 4+ years of proven experience.

 - Ability to build strong relationships with team members.

 - Strong analytical skills and a high capacity for conceptual thinking.

 - Experience and proficiency with Excel, or various accounting-related systems.

 - Familiarity with accounts payable processes and vendor management.

 - Ability to leverage trust to influence and drive outcomes without formal authority.

PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - CPA certification and experience with a Big 4 accounting firm are preferred.

 - Proven experience in setting up accounting processes for a new entity or business unit.

 - Proficiency in ERP systems (e.g., SAP, NetSuite) and advanced Excel.

 - Bilingual in English and Korean is preferred to facilitate group-level reporting.

WORK AUTHORIZATION

The ideal candidate for this role will have authorization to work in the US prior to joining TransGrid Energy LLC. The Company will not sponsor or offer assistance with the sponsorship for work authorization in the United States.

DIVERSITY AND INCLUSION

TransGrid Energy believes that diversity and inclusion are vital to our success. We believe that by embracing different ideas and approaches, we enhance our ability to deliver the best results for our employees, our environment, and ultimately our business partners and customers. We foster a culture where all colleagues are able to share their passions and ideas in order that we may collectively embrace and overcome the challenges in our industry, and work toward a brighter future.

TransGrid Energy is an Equal Opportunity / Affirmative Action Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gen

source_jobs.title: Staff Accountant
source_jobs.description:
COMPANY OVERVIEW

TransGrid Energy is a newly established renewable energy company to invest, develop, own and operate utility-scale renewable energy projects. TransGrid has a robust pipeline of projects in the near term, and it will expand its energy asset portfolio across the United States.

As an affiliate company of Hanwha Group, a FORTUNE Global 500 company driven by a firm commitment to advancing the clean energy future, TransGrid Energy is strived to shape the renewable energy landscape by providing sustainable and reliable power solutions tailored to meet the energy needs of today and tomorrow.

POSITION OVERVIEW

The Staff Accountant is a key member of the finance department, responsible for managing the day-to-day accounting operations, with a primary focus on accounts payable and accounts receivable. This role requires a detail-oriented professional who can ensure the accuracy and integrity of financial transactions while supporting the overall financial health of the company.

RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Process all accounts payable (AP), including vendor invoices, expense reports, and other financial transactions and following up on overdue accounts.

 - Process accounts receivable (AR), including invoicing customers, processing payments in a timely and accurate manner.

 - Reconcile vendor statements and customer accounts to resolve any discrepancies.

 - Assist with month-end and year-end closing procedures, including general ledger maintenance and various account reconciliations.

 - Collaborate with other departments to ensure proper authorization and accurate coding of invoices and expenses.

 - Maintain accurate financial records and documentation in compliance with company policies.

 - Contribute to the continuous improvement of financial and administrative processes.

 - Perform other general accounting and administrative duties as assigned.

REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - Bachelor’s degree in accounting, finance, or a related field

 - Bilingual (English & Korean)

 - 2+ years of proven experience.

 - Strong organizational skills and exceptional attention to detail.

 - Proficiency with Microsoft Office Suite, especially Excel.

 - Ability to work effectively both independently and as part of a team.

 - Experience with automated AP/AR systems.

PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - Familiarity with accounting software (e.g., QuickBooks, SAP, Beanworks).

 - Experience in the renewable energy or construction industry.

WORK AUTHORIZATION

The ideal candidate for this role will have authorization to work in the US prior to joining TransGrid Energy LLC. The Company will not sponsor or offer assistance with the sponsorship for work authorization in the United States.

DIVERSITY AND INCLUSION

TransGrid Energy believes that diversity and inclusion are vital to our success. We believe that by embracing different ideas and approaches, we enhance our ability to deliver the best results for our employees, our environment, and ultimately our business partners and customers. We foster a culture where all colleagues are able to share their passions and ideas in order that we may collectively embrace and overcome the challenges in our industry, and work toward a brighter future.

TransGrid Energy is an Equal Opportunity / Affirmative Action Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability or veteran status, age or any other federally protected class.

source_jobs.title: Power Markets Associate
source_jobs.description:
COMPANY OVERVIEW

TransGrid Energy is a renewable energy company focused on investing, developing, owning and operating utility-scale battery energy storage systems and PV projects. TransGrid has a robust pipeline of projects under construction and in late-stage development, and the Company is actively expanding its energy asset portfolio across the United States.

As an affiliate company of Hanwha Group, a FORTUNE Global 500 company driven by a firm commitment to advancing the clean energy future, TransGrid Energy is striving to shape the renewable energy landscape by providing sustainable and reliable power solutions tailored to meet the energy needs of today and tomorrow.

POSITION OVERVIEW

TransGrid Energy is seeking a highly analytical and technically capable Power Markets Associate to join its Asset Management team. This role will help turn large volumes of market, operational, and pricing data into actionable commercial intelligence that supports asset optimization, cash trading, risk awareness, and portfolio decision-making.

The Power Markets Associate will play a key role in building internal analytical infrastructure, automating reporting, improving transparency into market and asset performance, and supporting commercial strategy across TGE’s growing fleet. The role requires a strong mix of power-market understanding, quantitative rigor, data-management capability, and business judgment.

The ideal candidate combines strong SQL / Python skills with a practical understanding of ISO/RTO market dynamics, nodal pricing behavior, congestion, ancillary services, and the commercial drivers that matter to a portfolio of battery and solar assets.

RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Build and maintain internal data pipelines that ingest, clean, validate, and centralize market, weather, pricing, operational, and settlement data from external and internal sources.

 - Develop and manage scalable datasets and analytical tools that support asset optimization, cash trading, and portfolio-risk monitoring.

 - Query and analyze large time-series datasets to identify pricing patterns, congestion trends, basis exposure, volatility drivers, and other market signals relevant to TGE’s assets.

 - Automate recurring workflows and reporting processes using SQL, Python, and related tools to reduce manual effort and improve speed, consistency, and control.

 - Create dashboards and reports that provide internal stakeholders with clear visibility into market conditions, portfolio performance, and key commercial risk indicators.

 - Support trading and asset-management decisions through quantitative analysis, scenario evaluation, and market intelligence.

 - Assist with settlement validation, collateral awareness, and post-trade or post-dispatch performance analysis.

 - Support onboarding of new assets, markets, and data sources by helping establish reporting structures, commercial views, and monitoring frameworks.

 - Monitor evolving ISO/RTO market rules, regulatory developments, and emerging market opportunities affecting BESS, solar, and related commercial strategies.

 - Work closely with Asset Management, trading, operations, finance, and third-party counterparties to ensure analytical work supports practical commercial execution.

 - Contributes to the development of a disciplined, scalable internal analytics capability that supports TGE’s broader commercial buildout.

 - Perform other duties and special projects as assigned.

REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - Bachelor’s degree in business, economics, finance, engineering, mathematics, computer science, or a related field.

 - Minimum of 2 years of experience in power markets, asset optimization, commercial asset management, market operations, trading analytics, or related wholesale energy-market activity.

 - Strong SQL skills and demonstrated experience using Python, including libraries such as Pandas and NumPy, for data analysis, automation, and reporting.

 - Proven ability to work with large, compl

source_jobs.title: Project Development Manager
source_jobs.description:
COMPANY OVERVIEW

TransGrid Energy is a renewable energy company focused on investing, developing, owning and operating utility-scale battery energy storage systems and PV projects. TransGrid has a robust pipeline of projects under construction and in late-stage development, and the Company is actively expanding its energy asset portfolio across the United States.

As an affiliate company of Hanwha Group, a FORTUNE Global 500 company driven by a firm commitment to advancing the clean energy future, TransGrid Energy is striving to shape the renewable energy landscape by providing sustainable and reliable power solutions tailored to meet the energy needs of today and tomorrow.

POSITION OVERVIEW

The Development Manager is responsible for leading the full lifecycle development of projects from initial intake through delivery of EPC-ready packages. This role sits at the center of the development organization and is accountable for maturing and de-risking projects by advancing site control, interconnection, permitting, and engineering integration. The Development Manager works cross-functionally with the Engineering Solutions, Commercial, and Regulatory teams to ensure each project progresses through defined go/no-go gates and is positioned for successful handoff to Project Execution.

This role requires a broad understanding of grid-scale BESS and integrated energy systems, including behind-the-meter (BTM) energy solutions, microgrids, and distributed energy resources (DERs), as well as the ability to manage complex stakeholder environments and development timelines.

RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Lead development execution for assigned projects from intake through EPC readiness

 - Define and execute project development strategies, including risk identification and mitigation

 - Advance and secure site control, interconnection agreements, and permitting approvals

 - Integrate technical solutions into executable development plans

 - Coordinate across internal teams and external stakeholders

 - Drive go/no-go recommendations

 - Prepare projects for EPC handoff and maintain continuity through COD

QUALIFICATIONS

 - 7-12 years of experience in BESS, renewables, or data center development

 - Experience developing BTM energy systems, microgrids, or DER-integrated projects preferred

 - Strong knowledge of interconnection processes, permitting, and site control

 - Familiarity with data center or large-load development preferred

 - Experience managing cross-functional teams and external consultants

 - Demonstrated ability to manage complex schedules, budgets, and risk frameworks.

WORK AUTHORIZATION

The ideal candidate for this role will have authorization to work in the US prior to joining TransGrid Energy LLC. This role will not sponsor or offer assistance with the sponsorship for work authorization in the United States.

DIVERSITY AND INCLUSION

TransGrid Energy believes that diversity and inclusion are vital to our success. We believe that by embracing different ideas and approaches, we enhance our ability to deliver the best results for our employees, our environment, and ultimately our business partners and customers. We foster a culture where all colleagues are able to share their passions and ideas in order that we may collectively embrace and overcome the challenges in our industry, and work toward a brighter future.

TransGrid Energy is an Equal Opportunity / Affirmative Action Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability or veteran status, age or any other federally protected class.

notes: Renewable energy company with a focus on utility-scale battery energy storage systems and solar projects.
